gpsPaymentComponent.infoText: You will be redirected to our payment provider GPS for payment

cepasPaymentComponent.infoText:
A credit card payment processing fee of {{processingFee}} will be added to this transaction.

When using the credit card payment method, you agree to pay the full amount of {{totalAmount}} which includes this credit card processing fee.

You will be redirected to the State of Michigan's Central Electronic Payment Authorization System (CEPAS) Payment Module to enter your credit card information for processing. After your credit card transaction has been successfully completed, do not close the Internet browser window from the CEPAS website. You will be automatically redirected back to the Uniform Commercial Code website to complete your transaction. The Uniform Commercial Code website does not store personal credit card information.

filer.title: Title
filer.anonConcernInfo: While the Charities Regulator will accept anonymous concerns, it is difficult for us to investigate and respond effectively to information we receive this way
filer.dataProtect: The Charities Regulator takes appropriate precautions to protect the confidentiality of your personal information in accordance with Data Protection legislation. Personal information that you provide will only be used in line with the purpose for which you provided it and will not be shared with third parties unless allowed or required by law. The Charities Regulator may use your anonymised personal data for statistical purposes.

charityDetails.title: Charity details
charityDetails.entityName: Charity name (or entity representing itself as a charity)

concern.title: Details about your concern
concern.showDetailsOfPreviousConcern: Have you raised your concern with the charity?
concern.pleaseProvideDetails: If yes, please provide details. If no, why not?
concern.relationshipWithCharity: What is your relationship with the charity (employee, trustee, beneficiary, etc)
concern.issueDate: When did the issue you are concerned about happen?
concern.mainPointOfConcern: Please set out the main points of your concern? (Give enough detail for us to understand each point. For example, dates, and particulars of all parties involved)
concern.concernEvidance: Please set out what evidence you have to support your concerns (list below)
concern.otherRegulators: Have you approached any other regulator or enforcement body or An Garda Síochána with this concern? If so, please provide details.
concern.otherRegulatorsHint: Please note concerns relating to criminal activity – for example, theft or embezzlement – that are matters for An Garda Síochána should also be reported directly to them.
concern.caseAttachments: Please upload any attachment related to your case here

declaration.title: Declaration
declaration.note1: We have an evidence-based approach to our work and will test allegations as part of our assessment process before deciding on the most appropriate action to take.
declaration.note2: A person who knowingly supplies us with false information may be guilty of an offence under Section 63 (False Statements) of the Charities Act 2009.
declaration.note3: The information I have provided is true to the best of my knowledge.
declaration.knowledgeStatemenInd: I confirm the above statement is true

gpsPaymentComponent.infoText: You will be redirected to our payment provider GPS for payment

cepasPaymentComponent.infoText:
A credit card payment processing fee of {{processingFee}} will be added to this transaction.

When using the credit card payment method, you agree to pay the full amount of {{totalAmount}} which includes this credit card processing fee.

You will be redirected to the State of Michigan's Central Electronic Payment Authorization System (CEPAS) Payment Module to enter your credit card information for processing. After your credit card transaction has been successfully completed, do not close the Internet browser window from the CEPAS website. You will be automatically redirected back to the Uniform Commercial Code website to complete your transaction. The Uniform Commercial Code website does not store personal credit card information.

filer.title: Title
filer.anonConcernInfo: While the Charities Regulator will accept anonymous concerns, it is difficult for us to investigate and respond effectively to information we receive this way
filer.dataProtect: The Charities Regulator takes appropriate precautions to protect the confidentiality of your personal information in accordance with Data Protection legislation. Personal information that you provide will only be used in line with the purpose for which you provided it and will not be shared with third parties unless allowed or required by law. The Charities Regulator may use your anonymised personal data for statistical purposes.

charityDetails.title: Charity details
charityDetails.entityName: Charity name (or entity representing itself as a charity)

concern.title: Details about your concern
concern.showDetailsOfPreviousConcern: Have you raised your concern with the charity?
concern.pleaseProvideDetails: If yes, please provide details. If no, why not?
concern.relationshipWithCharity: What is your relationship with the charity (employee, trustee, beneficiary, etc)
concern.issueDate: When did the issue you are concerned about happen?
concern.mainPointOfConcern: Please set out the main points of your concern? (Give enough detail for us to understand each point. For example, dates, and particulars of all parties involved)
concern.concernEvidance: Please set out what evidence you have to support your concerns (list below)
concern.otherRegulators: Have you approached any other regulator or enforcement body or An Garda Síochána with this concern? If so, please provide details.
concern.otherRegulatorsHint: Please note concerns relating to criminal activity – for example, theft or embezzlement – that are matters for An Garda Síochána should also be reported directly to them.
concern.caseAttachments: Please upload any attachment related to your case here

declaration.title: Declaration
declaration.note1: We have an evidence-based approach to our work and will test allegations as part of our assessment process before deciding on the most appropriate action to take.
declaration.note2: A person who knowingly supplies us with false information may be guilty of an offence under Section 63 (False Statements) of the Charities Act 2009.
declaration.note3: The information I have provided is true to the best of my knowledge.
declaration.knowledgeStatemenInd: I confirm the above statement is true
